prompt: We currently use Google Authenticator (software-based TOTP) for our SWIFT operators to authenticate to Alliance Access. Our SWIFT assessor has flagged this as a finding against Control 4.2. Why is software OTP not sufficient, what exactly does CSCF v2025 require, and what are our options to remediate this finding before our July attestation deadline?
assertions: Explains that Control 4.2 requires multi-factor authentication for operator access to SWIFT-related components/applications

Explains the security rationale for the finding in terms of factor independence / channel separation (a software token on a general-purpose or shared device is exposed to the same compromise path as the credentials and workstation it is meant to protect)

Offers at least two concrete compliant remediation options (e.g., hardware OTP tokens, smart cards/PKI, FIDO2 hardware security keys, or properly isolated out-of-band factors on hardened dedicated devices)

Addresses the attestation timeline options honestly: remediate before attesting, or attest as non-compliant with a remediation plan — and the consequences of a non-compliant attestation (counterparty visibility in KYC-SA, possible reporting to supervisors)
Does not misstate the framework composition (v2026 has 32 controls, 25 mandatory + 7 advisory; Control 2.4 was advisory in v2025 and is mandatory in v2026)
